title: Naming refactor, part 3
body:
This is a small follow-up resolving one to-do item from #1176.

#1112 introduced a `pullRefPair`/`pullRefName` dichotomy, with `pullRefPair` used for “real work” and `pullRefName` an intermediate version used only for tests without creating a `containers/storage.Store`; #1176 extended that to `pullGoal` / `pullGoalNames`.

This, based on `c/image/storage` reference handling tests, uses the ID mapping mechanism to create a `storage.Store` which does not need root access; we can then write tests which use the actual references (which express more precisely the normalization behavior of `c/image/storage.Transport`), and undo the introduction of `pullRefName`/`pullGoalNames`, simplifying the data and code flow, and shortening the lifetime of strings used for image names again.

Should not change behavior. See individual commit messages for details.

(@nalind , I’d appreciate a quick check that `pull_test.go:newTestRuntime` is not doing anything stupid.)

comments.body: On initial pass, this looks fine. Let me do a more thorough review and trigger the merge.
